publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n01.npcDialogue: You've found me at a delicate hour. I have three bills before the assembly, a consul threatening religious obstruction, and half the forum convinced I'm either a savior or a traitor. So forgive my directness — what do you want, and how quickly can we conclude this?

options.playerDialogue: I want to help you pass the citizenship bill. Tell me what you need.
I want to understand your real strategy. The bills are the surface — what's underneath?
I want to warn you. The optimates are planning more than religious obstruction.

publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n02.npcDialogue: A volunteer. Refreshing, if suspicious. The citizenship bill distributes new Italian citizens across all thirty-five tribes instead of cramming them into a handful of meaningless ones. The optimates know that if the Italians vote across all tribes, the populares control the assembly permanently. That's why they'll do anything to stop it.

options.playerDialogue: Tell me where the resistance is strongest.

publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n03.npcDialogue: Perceptive. Yes, the citizenship bill is the mechanism, but the prize is the Mithridatic command. If I can transfer it from Sulla to Marius through the tribal assembly, Marius gets his war, the populares get their champion back, and Sulla loses his route to permanent power. Three problems, one vote.

options.playerDialogue: That's brilliant. And incredibly dangerous. Sulla won't accept it.
You're using the Italians as a tool. Do they know that?

publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n04.npcDialogue: Go on. I have ears in most places, but not all of them. What have you heard?

options.playerDialogue: They're talking about a senatus consultum ultimum. Emergency powers to shut you down by force.
Nothing specific yet. But the mood in the senate is uglier than usual.

publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n05.npcDialogue: Here's what I need — and I need it before the next assembly session. The consul Pompeius Rufus is going to declare a justitium, a suspension of all public business, to block the vote. I need that declaration discredited before it's made. How would you handle something like that?

responses.playerDialogue: Find the legal precedent that limits his authority to declare it. Beat him with his own rules.
Offer him something. Every consul has a price that isn't money — a legacy, a province, a favor for his son.
Fill the forum with enough citizens that a suspension becomes impossible to enforce. Make it a dead letter.
Leak word that his co-consul opposes the suspension. Make Pompeius think he's alone before he declares.

fallbackResponse.playerDialogue: I'm not sure I'm the right person for that kind of work.

publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n06.npcDialogue: You know less than you pretend. That's fine — most people in this city do. But I didn't survive three years of tribunician politics by trusting men who oversell their information. Let's talk about what you can actually do.

options.playerDialogue: Fair enough. What do you need?

publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n07.npcDialogue: Let me assess where we stand with each other.

publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n08.npcDialogue: Now you're thinking like a tribune. I have a network of freedmen and clients who can fill the forum on command. But I need someone who can coordinate them — someone the optimates won't immediately identify as mine. Are you willing to get your hands dirty in a way that can't be washed off?

options.playerDialogue: My hands are already dirty. Point me at the forum.
I'll coordinate, but I won't be seen. Plausible deniability for both of us.

publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n09.npcDialogue: Welcome to the game beneath the game. When the vote happens — and it will happen — you'll be in a position most men would kill for. Literally, in some cases. I'll send word when I need you.

publius_sulpicius_convo_0_n10.npcDialogue: I won't pretend we're allies. But I'll remember that you came to me, and that you listened. In this city, that's not nothing. Stay watchful — the vote is coming whether you're ready or not.

deltas.reason: Identified as part of the tribunician bloc
Operating in the shadows of Roman legislation
Sulpicius registers you as a possible future asset
